A total of 125 Indian athletes will compete at the 2026 Commonwealth Games, which will be held in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2. The squad includes 77 male and 48 female athletes across sports disciplines, including para sports.India will be represented across 13 disciplines - eight able-bodied sports and five para-sports - with hopes of adding to its rich Commonwealth Games medal tally.Indian athletes will compete in athletics, boxing, weightlifting, judo, artistic gymnastics, track cycling, bowls and swimming.The country's para-athletes will take part in para-athletics, 3x3 wheelchair basketball, para-powerlifting, para-swimming and para-track cycling.Olympic and world champion Neeraj Chopra will be one of India's biggest medal hopes in athletics.The track and field events will be held from July 27 to August 1 at Scotstoun Stadium. The men's javelin throw final is scheduled for August 1, where Neeraj will look to regain the Commonwealth Games gold medal.He won the title at Gold Coast 2018 but missed the Birmingham 2022 Games because of injury.Another major medal contender is Mirabai Chanu, who will compete in weightlifting. The weightlifting events are scheduled from July 26 to 30.The two-time Commonwealth Games champion will aim to add another gold medal to her collection in Glasgow.Here's the complete schedule of Indian athletes at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow.Stay Updated With: CWG Schedule
Article Summary: A total of 125 Indian athletes will compete at the 2026 Commonwealth Games. India will be represented across 13 disciplines - eight able-bodied sports and five para-sports. Olympic and world champion Neeraj Chopra will be one of India's biggest medal hopes in athletics.

Article Title: NEET's top 2 scorers: Protest is a right, reform is the answer
Article Text:
Panshul Bansal from Haryana, who has secured AIR 2 in NEET UG 2026

NEW DELHI: Aryan Gupta and Panshul Bansal felt the same anger and sense of injustice as lakhs of NEET-UG aspirants when the May 3 examination was cancelled after a paper leak. They believe students were within their rights to protest. However, instead of joining the demonstrations, they stepped away from the noise, revitalised their will and prepared for the June 21 re-examination.Both scored 715 out of 720, with Gupta securing AIR 1 and Bansal placed second under the tie-breaking rule. They now want computer-based testing, tighter security and two NEET attempts a year to help examinees avoid what they experienced this year."Although it was unfair, it was not unfair only to me. It was unfair to everyone," Gupta said, adding that he treated the retest as a chance to improve his performance. As reports about the leak and protests multiplied, Bansal stopped following the news for nearly two months as it was giving him "negative thoughts".Both defended students' right to demonstrate. "Protest and dissent are basic rights of a citizen. They are essential for a democracy," Gupta said. Bansal said his job was to prepare, while it was govt's responsibility to repair the system.
Article Summary: Aryan Gupta and Panshul Bansal scored 715 out of 720. Gupta secured AIR 1 and Bansal placed second under the tie-breaking rule. They now want computer-based testing, tighter security and two NEET attempts a year.

Article Title: '5,200 applications have been received' for CEO post: Ram Mandir Trust holds second meeting after donation row
Article Text:
Ram Mandir Trust holds second meeting after donation row

NEW DELHI: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust on Wednesday held its second meeting in Ayodhya since the alleged donation embezzlement case surfaced, reviewing administrative matters as the probe continues.The trust said it has received 5,200 applications for the post of CEO of the Ram Mandir, but has deferred the appointment by a month to allow the selection committee more time to scrutinise the applications. It also announced plans to set up a permanent institute to train and certify priests for the temple.Addressing a press conference after the meeting, trust treasurer Govind Dev Giri said the trust had decided to establish a permanent institute for training priests."We have decided to set up a permanent institute for training priests, who would be certified by the Ram temple trust," Giri said.He said the trust had received 5,200 applications for the CEO post."A total of 5,200 applications have been received for the Ram temple trust CEO post. Considering the volume of applications, the selection committee sought one month's additional time, which has been approved by the Trust," Giri said.He added that the trust would appoint a secretary on an interim basis to meet its growing administrative requirements until the CEO is selected.Interim general secretary Krishna Mohan said there was no discussion on appointing the CEO as the trust is still awaiting the report of the Special Investigation Team (SIT) probing the alleged donation embezzlement case."There was no discussion on the appointment of the CEO as the SIT report has not been received yet," Krishna Mohan said.The trust also announced the formation of a permanent committee to oversee the religious affairs of the Ram temple.According to Giri, the committee will comprise nine saints, including five from Ayodhya and four from outside, to supervise the temple's religious activities.The meeting comes weeks after the trust accepted the resignations of its former general secretary Champat Rai and trustee Anil Mishra following the alleged donation embezzlement case. Krishna Mohan was appointed interim general secretary, while an SIT was constituted to investigate the allegations.
Article Summary: Ram Mandir Trust holds second meeting since alleged donation embezzlement case. Trust says it has received 5,200 applications for the post of CEO of the Ram Mandir. Trust also announced plans to set up a permanent institute to train and certify priests for the temple.

Article Title: 'Chapter is behind me': Lalit Modi says he'll return to India soon
Article Text:
File photo: Lalit Modi (Picture credit: ANI)

Tribunal gives Lalit Modi major relief

Tribunal rejects key allegations

NEW DELHI: Former Indian Premier League ( IPL ) chairman Lalit Modi on Wednesday said he plans to return to India by the end of this year or early next year after securing major legal relief from the Appellate Tribunal under the Smugglers and Foreign Exchange Manipulators (Forfeiture of Property) Act (SAFEMA) in the 2009 IPL South Africa FEMA case.Speaking a day after the tribunal's verdict, Lalit said he felt vindicated after fighting the case for 16 years and claimed the ruling had established the truth behind his actions during the 2009 IPL season."I'm really happy with the verdict yesterday. It's really been a great day. Sixteen years I have fought and whatever I have been saying to the media and to everybody has finally emerged as the truth," he said."I'm really happy that the tribunal has ruled in my favour. I was only concerned with the well-being of the IPL and nothing and nothing at all. That is the most dear to me."He added that he now considers the legal battle over and is looking forward to returning to India."Now that chapter is behind me, I'm going to move on in my life and I'm looking forward to coming back to India. I will come back probably end of this year or early next year. My daughter is due with my grandson in October and hopefully everything will be well and I'll be back in India," Lalit said.The Appellate Tribunal under SAFEMA has granted significant relief to Lalit Modi and other appellants by setting aside most of the Enforcement Directorate's findings and penalties in the long-running FEMA case linked to the 2009 IPL tournament in South Africa.The tribunal set aside the ED's penalty order issued against Lalit and several others in the 15-year-old case related to foreign exchange transactions during the tournament.The case stemmed from the BCCI's decision to shift the 2009 IPL season to South Africa because of security concerns arising from India's general elections.The Enforcement Directorate had alleged that foreign exchange worth $49.86 million (over Rs 243 crore at current equivalent value) was transferred outside India without prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India.The ED issued FEMA show-cause notices in 2011 and penalty orders in 2018 against Lalit, former BCCI officials and others.They later challenged those orders before the SAFEMA Appellate Tribunal.The tribunal's 105-page order, issued on July 16 by a division bench comprising Chairman Justice Munishwar Nath Bhandari and Member Rajesh Malhotra, held that Lalit was "not in-charge of the affairs and otherwise he has been framed in the case without any material showing his responsibility.""The penalty imposed on Shri Lalit Modi is wholly erroneous is thus interfered," the tribunal said.In a statement issued on Tuesday through his lawyer, Lalit had welcomed the verdict, saying it reaffirmed "the importance of due process, objective legal analysis and the principle that allegations must ultimately be tested against evidence and law".The tribunal rejected the Enforcement Directorate's central contention by holding that the principal foreign remittances related to IPL 2009 were current account transactions rather than capital account transactions, while also finding that Lalit was neither responsible for BCCI's FEMA compliance nor vested with the financial authority alleged by the agency.
Article Summary: Former Indian Premier League ( IPL ) chairman Lalit Modi says he plans to return to India by the end of this year or early next year. Lalit secured major legal relief from the Appellate Tribunal under the Smugglers and Foreign Exchange Manipulators (Forfeiture of Property) Act. The case stemmed from the BCCI's decision to shift the 2009 IPL season to South Africa because of security concerns.

Article Title: 'When you fear failure, you play to save yourself': Shreyas Iyer's message to Team India ahead of Zimbabwe T20Is
Article Text:
Shreyas Iyer (BCCI Photo)

Shreyas Iyer's T20I batting record against teams

Opposition Matches Innings Runs Highest Score Average Strike Rate 100s 50s Ducks Fours Sixes Australia 4 4 73 53 24.33 146.00 0 1 1 6 3 Bangladesh 3 3 108 62 54.00 183.05 0 1 0 7 8 England 11 10 367 80* 45.87 150.40 0 3 0 32 11 Ireland 2 2 13 10 6.50 92.85 0 0 0 2 0 New Zealand 13 11 225 58* 25.00 124.30 0 1 1 14 10 South Africa 8 8 116 40 19.33 113.72 0 0 0 6 7 Sri Lanka 9 8 296 74* 59.20 149.49 0 3 1 28 9 West Indies 8 8 137 64 19.57 119.13 0 1 1 15 3

New country, new challenge for Iyer



NEW DELHI: After a disappointing tour of England and Ireland, India captain Shreyas Iyer has made it clear that he does not want his players to carry the fear of failure into the T20I series against Zimbabwe.The India skipper said players should not worry about what people outside the dressing room are saying and should instead focus on expressing themselves freely."I don't think they should fear failure because when you have such thoughts at the back of your mind, then you play to save yourself. When you don't have that fear, you can get the best out of yourself," Iyer said ahead of the first T20I against Zimbabwe."We don't have to think much about what people are saying outside. We have to stay in the present. The more we play good cricket and play together as a unit, the better it will be for the team," he said.The pressure is firmly on Iyer after India failed to win a single white-ball match on the England and Ireland tour.India lost the two-match T20I series 0-2 to Ireland before suffering a 0-4 defeat against England in the five-match series.Despite those results, Iyer believes the difficult phase has helped the team understand what it needs to improve.India are scheduled to play three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe, starting Thursday."We had a rough patch in England, but there were a lot of positives out of that. We got to know what we have to build as a team going forward, what kind of presence of mind, awareness and adaptability we need once we step onto the field," Iyer said.This is Iyer's first tour of Zimbabwe and he has never played a T20I in the country before. He admitted that adapting quickly to the conditions would be crucial."Some of us are playing here for the first time, so the quicker we adapt, the better results we will get," he said."It is definitely very challenging. We came yesterday, today is practice and tomorrow is the match. But it is fun, and if you perform well in these situations, you will definitely be happy with yourself. The results didn't go our way, but that has brought the team closer to each other. We've been having a lot of discussions about how we're going to play as a unit going forward," Iyer said."It's a great exposure because you don't get this often. This is a challenging phase, and the quicker you overcome it, the better you become as a team," the Indian captain said.
Article Summary: India captain Shreyas Iyer does not want players to carry fear of failure into T20I series against Zimbabwe. India failed to win a single white-ball match on the England and Ireland tour. This is Iyer's first tour of Zimbabwe and he has never played a T 20I in the country before.

Article Title: 'I'm sorry': Why Argentina's assistant coach Roberto Ayala apologised after FIFA World Cup final
Article Text:
PTI Photo

Chaos after the final whistle



Ayala apologises for his actions



NEW DELHI: Argentina assistant coach Roberto Ayala has apologised for his altercation with Spain midfielder Dani Olmo after the FIFA World Cup 2026 final, admitting that his actions were unacceptable. The ugly scenes unfolded after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time on Sunday to lift the World Cup title.FIFA has since launched an investigation into the post-match brawl.Tensions boiled over immediately after the final whistle as players and staff from both teams became involved in heated confrontations.Spain captain Rodri was seen arguing with Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi. Leandro Paredes was shown a red card after punching Gavi, who had run onto the pitch to celebrate Spain's victory.Ayala also became involved in the incident.Television footage showed the former Argentina defender approaching Dani Olmo before making contact with the Spain midfielder. Olmo chose not to retaliate.Speaking after the incident, Ayala admitted he had made a mistake and said he regretted losing control."Obviously I'm sorry. Because of my position, I can't allow a feeling, or what I might receive from the other party, to change my mood and my actions.I'm sorry, but for me, these are things that stay there and that's it," Ayala said in an interview with Valencia Capital Radio."For me, things need to be put behind us and left there. It was more of a push than anything else, it wasn't a punch like they're saying, and that's it. It was a reaction to something he said, but that's it. If I see him, I'll obviously apologise to him in person," he said.Ayala said emotions got the better of him during the chaotic scenes and accepted full responsibility for his behaviour."It's a shame, and you have to accept the facts and the things you've done on the field. When the game ends, what I see is a fight in the middle of the pitch, and we rush to go after our players, and it ends up like that because that's not who we are," he said.He added that his original intention was to stop the confrontation rather than become part of it."I take responsibility for what I did. My intention was to go and separate them, that was my intention, but sometimes things happen and emotions run high, but that's no excuse. And given my position, my behaviour has to be different, regardless of what I receive," he said.FIFA has opened disciplinary proceedings into the post-match brawl.The governing body's disciplinary committee has appointed a disciplinary and ethics prosecutor to investigate the incidents involving players and coaching staff.
Article Summary: Argentina assistant coach Roberto Ayala apologises for altercation with Spain midfielder Dani Olmo. The ugly scenes unfolded after Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time on Sunday to lift the World Cup title. Ayala said emotions got the better of him during the chaotic scenes and accepted full responsibility for his behaviour.

Article Title: Glasgow gold quest begins: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu lead India's medal hopes at Commonwealth Games 2026
Article Text:
Neeraj Chopra and Mirabai Chanu (Image credit: Agencies)





Sports excluded from Glasgow Commonwealth Games :



Why are these sport excluded?



India will begin its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow on Thursday with a compact yet accomplished 125-member contingent aiming to maximise its opportunities in a drastically trimmed programme. The Games, running from July 23Aug 2, feature just 10 sports, forcing India to recalibrate expectations after a highly successful Birmingham campaign four years ago.The Indian squad comprises 77 men and 48 women competing across eight able-bodied and five para disciplines. Athletics forms the backbone of the campaign with 32 competitors, while boxing and judo have 14 athletes each, weightlifting 12 and swimming five. Tokyo Olympic silver medallist Mirabai Chanu will be India’s flagbearer at the opening ceremony, with Tokyo bronze medallist and former world champion boxer Lovlina Borgohain serving as baton bearer.The overall Indian delegation comprises 190 members - 125 athletes, 51 coaches and support staff and 14 contingent officials.Unlike Birmingham, where India fielded 210 athletes and won 61 medals, including 22 gold, Glasgow presents a very different challenge. The programme has been cut from 19 sports in 2022 to 10, with medal-rich disciplines such as badminton, wrestling, hockey, table tennis, cricket and squash omitted.Those six sports accounted for 30 of India’s medals in Birmingham, sharply reducing the country’s traditional medal base.Despite the pared-down programme, India boasts several genuine gold contenders. Tokyo Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra returns to the CWG after missing Birmingham through injury and will renew his rivalry with Pakistan’s reigning Olympic and Commonwealth champion Arshad Nadeem. The field also includes Sri Lanka’s world leader Rumesh Pathirage. Chopra, the 2018 champion, remains India’s biggest athletics hope.Long jumper Murali Sreeshankar, a Birmingham silver medallist, and high jumper Sarvesh Kushare, who set a national record of 2.31 metres last month before finishing third at the Monaco Diamond League meeting, further strengthen India’s track and field ambitions. Other key names include Gurindervir Singh, Animesh Kujur, Parul Chaudhary, Tajinderpal Singh Toor and Gulveer Singh.India’s weightlifting challenge will again be led by Mirabai, who is chasing a fourth CWG medal and a third successive gold. The boxing squad includes proven contenders such as Lovlina, Sakshi Chaudhary, Preeti Pawar and Jaismine Lamboria.The contingent spans generations, with 49-year-old lawn bowler Dinesh Kumar Singh the oldest athlete and 16-year-old para cyclist Lisha Das the youngest. Competitions will be staged at Scotstoun Stadium, the Scottish Event Campus, Glasgow International Arena and Tollcross International Swimming Centre.With Ahmedabad set to host the centenary Commonwealth Games in 2030, Glasgow offers India not only a shot at medals but also a valuable learning opportunity before it welcomes the sporting community home.Shooting (63 gold medals won by India tilll date), table tennis (10 gold), wrestling (100+ overall medals, including 6 gold in 2022 Birmingham Games), field hockey (3 silver for men while women won gold in 2002, silver in 2006 and bronze in 2022), squash (women’s doubles gold in Glasgow 2014), women’s cricket (silver in 2022).Glasgow stepped in after the Australian state of Victoria withdrew citing escalating costs. Organisers trimmed the Games to 10 sports across four venues within an eight-mile radius to keep expenses under control. Victoria’s projected budget had ballooned to around 14,500 cr. Glasgow will operate on a leaner £150 million (around 1,750 cr) budget, with £100 million funded by the Commonwealth Sport.
Article Summary: India will begin its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow on Thursday. The Games, running from July 23Aug 2, feature just 10 sports, forcing India to recalibrate expectations after a highly successful Birmingham campaign four years ago. Tokyo Olympic silver medallist Mirabai Chanu will be India’s flagbearer at the opening ceremony.

Article Title: 'Chess is abstract, that's why it's very isolating': American GM Hans Niemann
Article Text:
American GM Hans Niemann (Image credit: X)

CHENNAI: American GM Hans Niemann, who has described Magnus Carlsen and elite chess organisers and sponsors as a “mafia” over what he sees as their scheming and high-handedness, has expressed sympathy for D Gukesh’s struggles as world champion.“Corrective action is really difficult in chess because when things go bad, people around you cannot really pinpoint mistakes or come up with a concrete plan,” said world No. 19 Niemann.“In other sports it is body positioning, force, technique etc that could set the tone for improvement. But no one can go inside a chess player’s mind. Chess is an abstract sport. That’s why it is very isolating.”That said, Niemann did not sugarcoat his assessment of Gukesh’s standing as a player. “The main reason why Ding Liren and Gukesh did poorly after becoming world champions is that they were never the best in the world, not even close to it. Yes, they had a string of great results etc. Hence the setback they suffered is not a major shock.”Niemann welcomed the removal of the rating qualification spot for the 2026 Candidates Tournament, arguing that the qualifiers through that route in the previous three cycles — Ding, Alireza Firouzja and Hikaru Nakamura — had found loopholes in the system and played easier games to secure their places.The 23-year-old also gave the thumbs-up to the new regulations awarding two Candidates spots through the Total Chess World Championship Tour and two for cumulative consistency across the two-year FIDE Circuit. “These slots are given via far more equitable and grand sample size (of games) than FIDE Grand Swiss and World Cup. Total Chess is the most democratic format of all the events,” he said.Niemann even suggested that Total Chess could become the sole world championship format if the number of annual qualifying events before the grand finale were increased from three to 10-12. “That would be the fairest World Championship format in tune with the times.” Niemann was accused by world No. 1 Magnus Carlsen of cheating in classical chess, an allegation he denied. The two later reached an out-of-court settlement.
Article Summary: American GM Hans Niemann has expressed sympathy for D Gukesh’s struggles as world champion. The 23-year-old also gave the thumbs-up to the new regulations awarding two Candidates spots through the Total Chess World Championship Tour.

Article Title: India vs Zimbabwe 1st T20I: Shreyas Iyer's side seek reset after Ireland and England defeats
Article Text:
India captain Shreyas Iyer and Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza (BCCI Photo)

After twin series defeats in Ireland and England, Shreyas Iyer ’s struggling T20 side looks to regain confidence in 3-match series vs ZimbabweThe last time India faced Zimbabwe in a T20I, it was on Feb 26 in a must-win clash at the 2026 T20 World Cup in Chennai. Reeling from a crushing defeat to South Africa that had left their campaign hanging by a thread, India responded in style, thrashing Zimbabwe by 72 runs before going on to retain the World Cup title.More than five months later, the two sides meet again in vastly different circumstances. India arrive in Harare after successive T20I series defeats in Ireland and England, with new captain Shreyas Iyer still searching for his first victory. The three-match series, beginning on Thursday, offers the world champions a chance to arrest their slide and restore confidence.Having lost six of his first seven matches as T20I captain, Iyer knows results need to change quickly.“We had a rough patch in England, but there were a lot of positives. We understood what we need to build as a team going forward and the kind of awareness and adaptability required at this level,” Iyer said on Wednesday.The spotlight will once again be on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Harare is the venue where the 15-year-old announced himself on the global stage with a stunning 175 off 80 balls against England in the U-19 World Cup final in Jan. His opening partner Abhishek Sharma also has happy memories here, having scored his maiden T20I hundred at the ground in 2024.Sooryavanshi, however, comes into the series under scrutiny after a difficult debut tour of England, where scores of 14, 13 and 15 highlighted his struggles against high-quality pace, particularly Jofra Archer. Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza urged patience with the youngster.“If a 15-year-old is playing international cricket, criticising him after two or three matches isn’t justified. Vaibhav is a supremely talented cricketer and could be a generational talent if managed well,” Raza said.The series is important for several Indian players seeking to revive their T20 careers. Vice-captain Tilak Varma will be keen to rediscover form after disappointing returns in Ireland and England, while Ishan Kishan and Rinku Singh, both back in the setup, will look to cement their places.India’s revamped pace attack, featuring Prince Yadav, the returning Mayank Yadav, Ashok Sharma and Yash Thakur, will also be under the scanner. Leg-spinner Ravi Bishnoi, meanwhile, will hope to iron out his back-foot no-ball issues while working with bowling coach for this series, former India leftarm spinner, Sunil Joshi.Zimbabwe enter the contest with confidence after recent wins over Sri Lanka and Bangladesh, as well as a memorable 23-run upset of Australia at the 2026 T20 World Cup. With the experienced Raza leading the charge and quicks Blessing Muzarabani and Richard Ngarava capable of exploiting home conditions, the hosts will fancy their chances of testing an out-of-sorts Indian side.Raza believes India’s ultra-aggressive approach could create opportunities for Zimbabwe to spring another surprise. For India, though, the priority is simple. Stop the slide and start winning again.
Article Summary: After twin series defeats in Ireland and England, Shreyas Iyer ’s struggling T20 side looks to regain confidence in 3-match series vs Zimbabwe. The last time India faced Zimbabwe in a T20I, it was on Feb 26 in a must-win clash at the 2026 T20 World Cup in Chennai.

Article Title: 'P*** Espanya': Spain's Ferran Torres World Cup mural vandalised in Barcelona
Article Text:
Ferran Torres World Cup mural vandalised in Barcelona

A mural celebrating Spain's World Cup-winning hero Ferran Torres was vandalised less than 24 hours after it appeared in Barcelona, according to a report from Spanish outlet Dairo AS.The artwork, created by Barcelona-based street artist TVBoy in the city's Gràcia neighbourhood, honoured Torres after he scored the winning goal in Spain's 2026 World Cup final victory over Argentina.The mural showed Torres kissing the World Cup trophy with the words "Making history" and two stars representing Spain's two World Cup titles. It also came after Torres celebrated the title wearing a hat that read "Make Spain Great Again."Soon after it was unveiled, the mural was painted over several times. The first graffiti read "P*** Espanya," before it was replaced with "Espanya".By Wednesday morning, the wall displayed the message "volem seleccions catalanes" ("we want Catalan national teams"), reflecting ongoing political tensions around Catalan identity.Barcelona City Council later stepped in, cleaning the wall and repainting it black.TVBoy said the mural had already made an impact before it was vandalised.Recalling a moment while he was painting, he said, "A girl passed by and shouted; 'Mom, it’s Ferran Torres!' I smiled. Then I understood that art also connects generations."Despite lasting little more than a day, the mural sparked conversation beyond football, highlighting how sport, art and politics often intersect in Barcelona.
Article Summary: Ferran Torres mural vandalised less than 24 hours after it appeared in Barcelona. The artwork was created by Barcelona-based street artist TVBoy. It honoured Torres after he scored the winning goal in Spain's 2026 World Cup final victory over Argentina.

Article Title: Daily Inspiration: How Caitlin Clark changed women’s basketball through fearless leadership, skyrocketing financial landscape, fiery competitiveness, media prominence, unapologetic cultural footprint,
Article Text:
Daily Inspiration: How Caitlin Clark changed women’s basketball through fearless leadership, skyrocketing financial landscape, fiery competitiveness, media prominence, unapologetic cultural footprint, unmatched authenticity, and record breaking shots (Image via Getty: Caitlin Clark)

WNBA player Caitlin Clark’s main focus is winning championships

Unmatched authenticity, fearless leadership and unapologetic cultural footprint

Skyrocketing financial landscape and media prominence

Caitlin Clark’s role in boosting betting interest in women’s sports

What is the Caitlin Clark effect?

Record breaking shots of Caitlin Clark showing fiery competitiveness

Fearless leadership and unmatched authenticity

Education is power. Taylor Swift transformed the music industry not only through her talent but also through her understanding of business, branding, and marketing.In a similar way, longtime Swift fan and WNBA star Caitlin Clark has transformed women’s basketball through her impact both on and off the court.Swift’s passion has always been music, but her business acumen helped turn that passion into one of the world’s most successful entertainment brands.Likewise, Clark’s influence has extended beyond basketball, with her arrival widely credited for dramatically increasing the Indiana Fever’s valuation and boosting the WNBA’s popularity.Selected first overall by the Fever in the 2024 WNBA Draft, Clark has already built an impressive resume over three seasons [with 2026 being the third], earning numerous individual honors.While she is still chasing her first WNBA championship, she captured the 2025 WNBA Commissioner’s Cup title, even though she missed the championship game due to a groin injury, and has won three gold medals with USA Basketball junior national teams.“One of the most important things somebody ever said to me is you’ve reached this point, but as you continue to grow, the thing you have to do is be able to win,” Clark told Yahoo Sports in May 2026.“I feel like that’s the point I’m kind of at now. You have to be able to win. That’s my main focus, winning and winning a championship.Everything else will come along with that.” There’s her fiery competitiveness.Clark has officially declined to participate in the 2026 WNBA 3-Point Contest during All-Star Weekend in Chicago.She is reportedly prioritizing rest, health management, and recovery from a lingering back injury.The 2026 WNBA regular season is scheduled to end in September.According to WNBA official website, the Indiana Fever hold the second spot in Eastern Conference Division, right behind the Atlanta Dream, and hold the sixth spot in the overall league standings with 16 wins and 10 losses, as of this publication.USA Today’s Christine Brennan told The Excerpt in July 2025 that she really understood Caitlin Clark’s impact when she was looking at Clark’s play for the first time as an observer [and not as a journalist] from her kitchen in Washington, D.C.Clark was playing for Iowa in February 2023. “Can Indiana really beat Iowa? And I knew Clark was on the team,” Brennan recalled.“Well, the ending, buzzer beater, Clark’s flinging in from the parking lot, she’s sideways, her legs are askew, and the ball goes in, and she wins the game.”Everyone around Brennan watching with her couldn’t believe what had just happened.It helped USA Today’s Christine Brennan understand how fascinating Clark was.“Yes, she’s a basketball player, but she’s really an entertainer. She’s the high-wire act,” Brennan said.“Once you understand that, you can begin to understand the fascination that the nation has with her.”According to SportsData.io, Caitlin Clark’s entry into the WNBA has made women’s basketball a “must-watch” sport.Approximately 400,000 fans attended May 2024 games, marking it the highest-attended opening month in 26 years.More than half of all WNBA games were sellouts, a 156% increase from the 2023 season. Arenas reached an impressive 94% capacity, up 17% from the 2023 season.2024 WNBA season brought unmatched viewership, with games averaging 1.32 million viewers across ESPN, ESPN2, ABC, and CBC.It almost tripled the 2023 WNBA season’s average of 462,000 viewers.The opening-night match between the Indiana Fever and the Connecticut Sun drew 2.12 million viewers across ESPN2, ESPN+, and Disney+, peaking at 2.34 million.It became the most-watched WNBA game on Disney platforms ever.The Fever vs. Sun game averaged 1.56 million viewers on ESPN, making it the most-viewed WNBA game on ESPN ever.Social media and viral highlights which featured Clark and other top rookies drew engagement, with a 613% increase in monthly active users on the WNBA app.According to SportsData.io, Penn Entertainment, operator behind ESPN BET, reported a more than 150% surge in WNBA betting handle year-over-year.BetMGM saw a 108% increase in WNBA betting for 2024. Rithmm, a sports betting analytics tool, recorded a 180% increase in WNBA bets placed by its community members.Her presence has also contributed to the growth of women’s basketball fantasy leagues, which in turn provide new opportunities for fan interaction with the WNBA.WNBA merchandise sales increased 236% year-over-year, with Clark, Angel Reese, and Cameron Brink ranking in the top 5 for jersey sales during the first week of the 2024 season.Lucrative sponsorships include high-profile brands like Nike, which has reportedly signed a contract valued at $28 million with Clark.Victor Matheson, a professor of economics at the College of Holy Cross who specializes in the economic impact of sports, said in January 2025,“Looking at the typical attendance at games without Caitlin Clark and compared to games with Caitlin Clark, we can attribute roughly one out of every six tickets being sold in the WNBA to the ‘Caitlin Clark Effect.’”“It’s pure joy in the arenas she plays in,” said Debbie Antonelli, an NCAA basketball and WNBA analyst for ESPN and ACC Network.“Four franchises in the WNBA moved their home games to accommodate Fever games;that’s often double, triple or sometimes quadruple what [attendance] they would have gotten if they stayed in their arena.”NCAA basketball and WNBA analyst Debbie Antonelli describes Caitlin Clark as the Tiger Woods of women’s basketball.“Clarkenomics, I coined the phrase last November because Caitlin is the greatest economic disrupter in the history of [women’s basketball],” Antonelli said.“They weren’t so sure about Tiger. His celebrity and his following increased all the purses, and he was the disruptor in golf.When those things happen, they make people take a look at how and why they are doing things. And so that is what Caitlin has done.”Clark made WNBA history on July 17, 2026, when she recorded 45 points and 10 assists in a 110-107 victory for the Indiana Fever over the Seattle Storm.She has a habit of changing the dynamics of a game in the last few minutes, and that’s what she did this time too.Both teams were tied at 102 points with the final 60 seconds remaining in the fourth quarter, when she hit six three-pointers to help the Fever snatch a late victory.“Some nights you know you have it, even before the ball tips,” said Clark. “I felt like it was going to be one of those for me.I feel like you just have to believe you’re going to have those types of nights, and you have to visualize those types of nights. And I feel like that’s what I did.”Clark’s success has a huge impact on young girls. It provides a “You Can Do This” image to them. It increases their willingness to participate in sports and stay in them.David Berri, a professor of economics at Southern Utah University who spent the last two decades researching sports and economics, said,“There is a big problem with girls getting out of sports because they just don’t see a future in it.”Clark is the reason why young girls know the WNBA and will willingly participate in sports and believe that women have flourishing futures in sports.
Article Summary: Caitlin Clark has transformed women’s basketball through her impact both on and off the court. Her arrival has been widely credited for dramatically increasing the Indiana Fever's valuation and boosting the WNBA's popularity. Clark has declined to participate in the 2026 WNBA 3-Point Contest during All-Star Weekend.

Article Title: CWG 2026 Opening Ceremony Live Streaming: When and where to watch Commonwealth Games live on TV and online
Article Text:
Commonwealth Games 2026 (PTI Photo)

Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony: Date, Time and Venue

What Makes This Opening Ceremony Different?

Commonwealth Games 2026: Where to watch the CWG opening ceremony?

International broadcasters include:

United Kingdom: Channel 5 (opening ceremony) and TNT Sports/HBO Max

Canada: CBC and CBC Gem

Australia: 7Plus

NEW DELHI: The 2026 Commonwealth Games officially get underway on July 23, with Glasgow hosting the multi-sport event in a revamped format.This edition features 10 sports and athletes from 74 Commonwealth nations, making it a more compact competition than previous Games.India has sent a 125-member contingent, including para-athletes, and will look to build on its record haul of 61 medals from the 2022 edition.While Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra is expected to be one of India's biggest medal hopes, the absence of sports such as badminton, table tennis and shooting could impact the country's medal tally.Before the competition begins, the opening ceremony will welcome athletes and fans with a celebration of Scottish culture and the spirit of the Commonwealth.The opening ceremony is scheduled for Thursday, July 23, and will begin at 11:30 PM IST (7:00 PM local time).Unlike previous editions, the event will be staged at the OVO Hydro in Glasgow, Scotland, becoming the first Commonwealth Games opening ceremony to take place entirely inside an indoor arena.Organisers have promised a more immersive experience instead of the traditional stadium spectacle. The ceremony will feature performances, large-scale visual effects and the arrival of all 74 individually designed King's Batons, one representing each participating nation.India's contingent will be led by two Olympic medallists. Mirabai Chanu will carry the Indian national flag during the Parade of Nations. Lovlina Borgohain will carry the ceremonial King's Baton.The official mascot for Glasgow 2026 is Finnie, a unicorn inspired by Scotland's national animal.Fans in India can watch the ceremony live on Sony Sports Network, while live streaming will be available on Sony LIV.The opening ceremony will officially kick off the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games before athletes begin competing for medals across the 10 sporting disciplines.Stay Updated With: CWG Schedule
Article Summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games officially get underway on July 23, with Glasgow hosting the multi-sport event in a revamped format. India has sent a 125-member contingent, including para-athletes, and will look to build on its record haul of 61 medals from the 2022 edition.Unlike previous editions, the event will be staged at the OVO Hydro in Glasgow, becoming the first Commonwealth Games opening ceremony to take place entirely inside an indoor arena.

Article Title: Rohit Sharma IN; Sanju Samson and Tilak Varma OUT: Ex-India cricketer picks India squad for 2027 ODI World Cup
Article Text:

Batters

Wicketkeepers

Gill closes in on World No. 1

Poll Vote & Share your view Is Rohit Sharma's inclusion in the 2027 ODI World Cup squad justified? Yes, he has vast experience. No, his recent performance is concerning. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI Former India opener Abhinav Mukund has picked his 15-man India squad for the 2027 ODI World Cup, making some interesting selection choices.Mukund, who represented India in seven Tests between 2011 and 2017, has backed veteran opener Rohit Sharma despite recent speculation over his ODI future. However, Sanju Samson and Yashasvi Jaiswal failed to make the cut.The former cricketer unveiled his squad on ESPNcricinfo's YouTube channel.Mukund has gone with a blend of experience and youth, while placing his faith in a pace attack spearheaded by Jasprit Bumrah and Mohammed Siraj.: Shubman Gill (captain), Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli, Shreyas Iyer: KL Rahul, Ishan Kishan: Hardik Pandya, Axar Patel, Washington Sundar: Kuldeep Yadav: Jasprit Bumrah, Mohammed Siraj, Prasidh Krishna, Harshit Rana and Gurnoor BrarMeanwhile, Shubman Gill has moved within touching distance of the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC ODI batting rankings released after the England series.Gill scored 188 runs in the three-match series and is now just one rating point behind New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell, who leads the rankings with 802 points.Virat Kohli (767) and Rohit Sharma (758) are placed third and fourth respectively, giving India three batters in the top five.England's Joe Root, who was named Player of the Series after scoring an unbeaten 249 runs, jumped four places to eighth.Ben Duckett climbed 11 spots to joint-19th, while Jacob Bethell rose five places to 64th.India's newly appointed T20I captain Shreyas Iyer moved up two places to 13th in the ODI batting rankings.Among bowlers, Kuldeep slipped one place to 11th, with Afghanistan's Rashid Khan continuing to lead the rankings. England fast bowler Jofra Archer climbed to third after taking five wickets against India in the ODI series.
Article Summary: Abhinav Mukund has picked his 15-man India squad for the 2027 ODI World Cup. Mukund backed veteran opener Rohit Sharma despite recent speculation over his ODI future. Shubman Gill has moved within touching distance of the No. 1 spot in the latest ICC ODI batting rankings.

Article Title: Logan Paul pitched to play a WWE match against Conor McGregor following the UFC star's recent defeat
Article Text:
Logan Paul pitched for a WWE match with Conor McGregor (AFP Photo)

Logan Paul teases a plan for a WWE match against Conor McGregor as his next opponent

IMPAULSIVE

WWE superstar Logan Paul said that he has pitched a WWE match against Conor McGregor following the UFC star's latest defeat. The star revealed that he has recently contacted WWE officials with the idea of bringing McGregor into the WWE ring. The proposal came after McGregor took a dig at Roman Reigns, and Paul noted that he sees the Irish fighter shifting into WWE soon. If WWE hosts a match between McGregor and Logan Paul, it would certainly be a major attraction for both UFC and WWE fans. WWE star Logan Paul revealed that he is planning for a WWE match against Conor McGregor as his next opponent, following his recent UFC loss. The discussion came up when he invited UFC Bantamweight Champion Sean O’Malley on hispodcast. During the conversation, they addressed Conor McGregor’s return and his shocking loss to Max Holloway at UFC 329 earlier this month.It is to be noted that McGregor returned to fight after nearly five years away from competition. During the fight, he suffered another serious leg injury. This setback has raised fresh doubts about his return to MMA.Taking a jibe at McGregor, Paul stated that he is no more capable of fighting in the octagon following his injury. He also referred to McGregor’s tiff with Roman Reigns, when the OTC made an appearance at UFC Freedom 250.Paul noted that he sees the Irish fighter shifting into WWE after his recent loss.“Something I find so funny is, he definitely has roasted the WWE and WWE superstars in the past. He put out a Tweet dissing Roman Reigns in the past. … But like, bro, at the moment, Conor is doing WWE. Because he is incapable of fighting," Paul stated during the interview on his YouTube channel.Speaking further, Logan Paul revealed that he has pitched the idea of fighting McGregor to a WWE official, “I don’t think that WWE will let me fight anyone. I mean, I’ll be honest with you. I texted my boss after Conor lost. I was like, ‘Bro, Conor’s cooked. Like, do something. Do something with it.’ Like, make a bag. Let me fight him. Big WWE star vs. big WWE star, because that’s what he is now.’ No, he said he thinks he’s done," the WWE star stated.Paul added that while McGregor has accomplished incredible things in combat sports, he no longer believes the Irishman can compete at the highest level in MMA, given his injury and health status.In addition to this, earlier, McGregor also addressed the possibility of stepping into a WWE ring. He described WWE and mixed martial arts as separate forms of entertainment with different performers, adding that his feature in the ring would draw a lot of attention from the fans, making a huge crossover between MMA and WWE.Although nothing has been confirmed so far regarding Paul's proposal. But the idea has already sparked discussion among fans, given McGregor's hint to move to professional wrestling soon.
Article Summary: WWE star Logan Paul revealed that he is planning for a WWE match against Conor McGregor as his next opponent. The proposal came after McGregor took a dig at Roman Reigns. Paul noted that he sees the Irish fighter shifting into WWE after his recent loss.

The road to the Biggest WWE party of the Summer, SummerSlam 2026. The two-night Premium Live Event has been scheduled to take place at the US Bank Stadium in Minneapolis, Minnesota, on August 1 and 2. However, Gunther has pushed him to the limit with his constant attacks, and he will trade his suit for wrestling trunks for one night at SummerSlam.Aldis will square off against The Ring General in a singles match, which will be his debut performance at SummerSlam.Danhausen has proven to be an exciting addition since his arrival in WWE in February this year. Moreover, he has been the troublemaker for The Judgment Day members recently. This will see him square off against Dirty Dominik Mysterio in a singles match at SummerSlam. With Mr. Very Nice Very Evil being a weekly captivating force with his comedic antics, he has earned his debut at SummerSlam this year.Out of all the debutants this year at WWE SummerSlam, Oba Femi has the biggest match in his first-ever showing at the Biggest Party of the Summer. The Ruler is set to collide with Brock Lesnar in his hometown inside Hell in a Cell. Article Title: "This is huge": Matt Miller's hardest chapter yet grows even more complicated after the insider claims a "huge" discovery
Article Text:
Matt Miller's hardest chapter yet grows even more complicated after the insider claims a "huge" discovery(Getty)

Tony Farmer points to old podcast clip as investigation into Matt Miller's charity leagues continues



Matt Miller has spent the past few weeks focusing on recovery after the June 2026 accident that cost him his left arm. But even as the ESPN draft analyst works toward healing, the controversy surrounding his charity fantasy football leagues refuses to fade.Recently, the story took another turn. Independent investigator Tony Farmer said he uncovered what he called a "huge" breakthrough involving Miller's brother, Marshal Miller. While the claims remain unverified, they have added fresh questions as the Missouri Attorney General's Office continues its investigation into allegations surrounding Miller's fantasy leagues.Tony Farmer shared his latest findings in a series of posts on X after revisiting a 2021 episode of the Mic'd Up podcast. He claimed the recording included Marshal Miller acknowledging that he used the online username "EmmandEmm.""Holy crap, I found it," Farmer wrote on X. "Matt Miller's brother Marshal acknowledging on tape he goes by 'EmmandEmm.' This is huge."Farmer argued the username matters because "EmmandEmm" allegedly appeared in Matt Miller's charity fantasy football leagues. He also claimed another account, "MelloEsquire," belonged to Marshal Miller.According to registration records Farmer reviewed, "EmmandEmm" entered 19 leagues during the 2024 and 2025 seasons. He also alleged "MelloEsquire" registered for 14 leagues in 2024 and another 38 in 2025.Farmer said the two usernames appeared together in the same league 12 times. Since Matt Miller also played in those leagues, Farmer questioned whether the Miller brothers may have controlled multiple teams in the same contests.He also raised concerns about the league entry fees. Each participant reportedly paid $100 to enter, with half of that amount advertised as going to charity."Do you think Marshal was paying registration fees?" Farmer wrote on X. "If the answer is no, then it raises questions about the actual amount of money that went to charity vs. what was promised."The Missouri Attorney General's Office has not confirmed Farmer's latest claims, and no evidence has been publicly released showing that any registration fees went unpaid.The investigation began after several people alleged they never received prize money from Miller's fantasy leagues. Others claimed they paid for scouting sessions that never happened or continued paying subscription fees for scouting content that was not updated regularly. No criminal charges have been filed, and the investigation remains ongoing.Meanwhile, Matt Miller continues to recover from his injuries. In an update posted on X earlier this month, he said doctors successfully completed surgery to remove his left arm while saving his left leg after multiple procedures."A quick update: The surgery to remove my left arm was successful with hopes of an eventual prosthetic replacement," Miller wrote on X. "To best focus on my healing and recovery, I'm stepping away indefinitely and will be placed on leave from ESPN."Miller's GoFundMe campaign has raised about $51,000, including a $5,525 donation from Pat McAfee. While Miller focuses on rehabilitation, the investigation into his charity leagues continues, and Farmer's latest claims have added another layer of scrutiny to an already closely watch.
Article Summary: Independent investigator Tony Farmer said he uncovered what he called a "huge" breakthrough. He claimed the recording included Marshal Miller acknowledging that he used the online username "EmmandEmm" The Missouri Attorney General's Office has not confirmed Farmer's latest claims.

Article Title: Why did Caitlin Clark receive a technical foul tonight against Sun? Is she closer to suspension?
Article Text:
Caitlin Clark. Image via: Lucas Peltier-Imagn Images via Reuters Connect

Caitlin Clark received a technical foul against the Sun

Is Caitlin Clark closer to suspension?

Indiana Fever secured a stunning victory with a franchise record

How many points did Caitlin Clark score tonight?

Stat Total Points 27 Minutes 27 Field goals 8-of-13 Three-pointers 5-of-9 Free throws 6-of-6 Assists $11 Rebounds 2 Steals 1 Turnovers 6

Caitlin Clark's technical foul trouble followed her into the WNBA All-Star break. The Indiana Fever guard picked up the seventh technical of her season during Wednesday's 123-88 win over the Connecticut Sun, an incident that leaves her one infraction away from a mandatory one-game suspension.The tech came in the third quarter, part of a blowout that also gave Indiana a new franchise scoring record. Clark still finished with 27 points and 11 assists, but the foul overshadowed an otherwise dominant night for the Fever heading into their nine-day layoff.With under a minute left in the third quarter and Indiana up big, Clark was fouled on a drive by Connecticut's Saniya Rivers and then by Olivia Nelson-Ododa. Clark and Rivers exchanged words afterward, with Clark pointing toward the scoreboard, which read 95-67 in the Fever's favor.She told Rivers to check the score, and both players were hit with technical fouls in the ensuing double-tech. "Check the f****** scoreboard! Check the scoreboard," Clark reportedly said (per SI ). Teammate Sophie Cunningham pulled Clark away from the exchange. Clark did not return in the fourth quarter, and the WNBA could still rescind the technical if it rules the call unwarranted.Following the game, Clark said, "I thought we shared the ball really well. We played up tempo, and everybody had one of those nights where the ball was going through the hoop.""The league's technical foul rules are strict. Players face an automatic one-game suspension once they reach eight technicals in a season, with an additional suspension for every tech after that. Clark now sits at seven, tied with Atlanta's Angel Reese for the most in the WNBA this year. With 17 games remaining on Indiana's schedule, a suspension at some point looks increasingly likely unless the Fever successfully appeal Wednesday's call.The technical foul was a footnote in an otherwise historic night. Indiana's 123 points broke the franchise record of 114, set in an overtime win over the Chicago Sky on June 11, and tied the WNBA record for 100-point games in a season at 11. The Fever shot 61.6% from the field and 50% from three-point range while outrebounding Connecticut 47-35. Kelsey Mitchell added 23 points, and Aliyah Boston scored 22 as three Fever starters reached 20 or more.Clark entered the night averaging 20.7 points, 7.8 assists and 3.9 rebounds, and she topped her scoring average while dishing out three more assists than usual, all in a night shortened by the fourth-quarter benching.Wednesday marked Indiana's last game before the All-Star break. The Fever don't play again until July 28, giving both the team and the league office time to decide whether Clark's seventh technical stands. If it does, one more infraction anywhere in the season's final stretch ends her availability for at least a game.
Article Summary: Caitlin Clark picked up her seventh technical foul of the season during Wednesday's win. The Indiana Fever guard is one infraction away from a mandatory one-game suspension. The tech came in the third quarter, part of a blowout that also gave Indiana a new franchise scoring record.

Article Title: NFL trade rumors: Cleveland Browns could make shocking $52.5 million decision as Chiefs and Bears emerge among top landing spots
