Could Cleveland actually pull the trigger?

The Cleveland Browns have not put Jerry Jeudy on the trade block, but the veteran receiver is quickly becoming one of the realistic names to watch ahead of 2026. The recent analysis by Anthony Palacios of LWOS said that if Cleveland's season unravels, the franchise could be moving the former Pro Bowler rather than carrying another expensive veteran through a rebuilding phase.The NFL trade rumors come after the Browns invested heavily in youth during the 2026 NFL Draft, acquiring WRs Denzel Boston and KC Concepcion while continuing to reshuffle the offense under new head coach Todd Monken. Although no confirmation has been made from the team's end, Palacios said the Kansas City Chiefs, Chicago Bears and Carolina Panthers would make logical destinations should Cleveland decide to listen to offers.The rumor is less about Jeudy's talent and more about where the Browns stand as a franchise. Cleveland used premium draft capital on Boston and Concepcion, two receivers viewed as long-term building blocks. Combined with Todd Monken taking over the offense, the team appears committed to getting younger while evaluating quarterbacks Shedeur Sanders and Deshaun Watson.That’s why Anthony Palacios' projection comes into play. Rather than suggesting an active trade is being discussed, Palacios argues that another disappointing season could compel Cleveland into difficult decisions.If the Browns find themselves outside the playoff picture midway through the year, moving Jeudy for valuable draft capital could make more sense than keeping a veteran receiver on a team still searching for long-term stability.Also, Tony Grossi believes Jerry Jeudy could be a perfect candidate before the deadline. He said, "Jeudy could be an attractive target if a contending team loses a WR1 or WR2 prior to the NFL trade deadline of November 10. For that to happen, Jeudy must return to the form of his formidable 2024 season, which isn’t a stretch under Monken, who takes a hands-on approach to coaching receivers.He further added, “A good first half of the season by Jeudy, and the others, would definitely motivate Berry to pursue a trade. The market for receivers, however, wouldn’t net more than a third-round pick, if that much, depending on the contender’s desperation.”Jerry Jeudy is far from an expiring rental. After arriving from Denver, he signed a three-year, $52.5 million extension that keeps him under contract through the 2027 season.His contract is significant but not overwhelming by today's wide receiver market. For contenders looking to add another proven pass catcher, Jeudy's deal is manageable, especially considering he is still just 27 years old.Jerry Jeudy's 2025 numbers didn't match the breakout campaign he enjoyed the previous year, but his overall resume remains attractiveAlso, his career totals entering the 2026 season are as follows:The sudden dip in performance coincided with Cleveland's offensive struggles, but Jeudy has already shown he can play at a high level when featured in the passing game.Among the rumored teams, Kansas City arguably stands out the most. According to Palacios, the Kansas City Chiefs still need another receiver around Patrick Mahomes. While Xavier Worthy continues to develop, the receiving corps remains relatively young, and uncertainty surrounding Rashee Rice's future has only fueled outside speculation about Kansas City's long-term plans.Jeudy would immediately give Andy Reid another polished route runner capable of winning one-on-one matchups. His versatility to play both outside and in the slot would also allow the Chiefs to move receivers around more freely, creating favourable matchups for Mahomes. For a team with Super Bowl expectations every season, adding an experienced receiver like Jeudy could raise the ceiling of the offense.The Chicago Bears have spent the past two offseasons building around Caleb Williams. They are expected to depend on Rome Odunze, Luther Burden and rookie Zavion Thomas, but that group still lacks a veteran receiver with Jeudy's experience. His route-running and ability to create separation would give Williams another reliable target while taking pressure off the younger pass catchers. If Chicago believes it is ready to compete immediately, adding an established receiver could accelerate that process.The Carolina Panthers have surrounded Bryce Young with more weapons than ever before. The Panthers already feature Tetairoa McMillan, Jalen Coker and Chris Brazzell, while Xavier Legette still has something to prove entering another season. If the Panthers think they need another trustworthy WR to fill the offence, Jeudy becomes a good option. His previous stats prove that he could help a young passing attack while giving Bryce Young another dependable target in key situations.As of now, this remains exactly what it is: a rumor. Neither the Cleveland Browns nor Jeudy have said that a trade is in line, and there have been no reports that hint at active negotiations. But Palacios' shows a realistic scenario based on Cleveland's current roster health, recent draft strategy and long-term outlook.If Boston and Concepcion emerge quickly and the Browns fail to stay in the AFC playoff race, Jerry Jeudy could become one of the league's most rightful trade picks before the deadline.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns have not put Jerry Jeudy on the trade block, but the veteran receiver is quickly becoming one of the realistic names to watch ahead of 2026. The Kansas City Chiefs, Chicago Bears and Carolina Panthers would make logical destinations should Cleveland decide to listen to offers.

Article Title: What did Sophie Cunningham say about trans athlete in women’s sport? New controversy surrounds Fever guard
Article Text:
Sophie Cunningham. Image via: USA TODAY Network via Reuters Connect

What did Sophie Cunningham say about trans athletes?

WNBA and teammates have weighed in before

Sophie Cunningham previously commented on trans athletes as well

Sometimes, it’s her viral meme, or her stunning appearance, sometimes her on court feuds or her blunt commments; Sophie Cunningham always keep her name in the biggest WNBA discussions. Sophie Cunningham is once again at the center of the trans athlete debate after ESPN's Hallie Grossman published a profile on the Indiana Fever guard July 21 that dug into her views on transgender participation in women's sports. Cunningham told ESPN she has been mischaracterized as anti-trans, saying instead that her goal is protecting girls and young women in athletics from competing against those she describes as biological men.The comments arrive as Cunningham, one of the WNBA's most recognizable and polarizing players, continues to push back on the "MAGA Barbie" label attached to her. They also land during a season in which the Fever's locker room has been drawn repeatedly into national political fights, largely tied to the treatment of teammate Caitlin Clark.Cunningham told ESPN, "I got a lot of negative feedback about me hating trans."She went on to frame her position as one rooted in protecting young female athletes from facing what she called biological men in competition, while insisting she isn't trying to alienate anyone."I think that I am here to extend love. But I also think with that love is truth, being honest. And I want to protect young girls in a locker room, or young girls in sport who shouldn't have to go against biological men."On her broader political identity, she pushed back against being boxed in, saying she holds views from both sides of the aisle and resents assumptions made about her based on her image and background.Grossman's profile noted that Cunningham feels qualified to weigh in on the subject because of her own background as a young female athlete. The Missouri native, now in her second season with Indiana, is averaging 9.4 points per game on 48 percent shooting.The debate is not new to the Fever roster. Former Cunningham teammate Brianna Turner, now with the Las Vegas Aces, wrote an op-ed for USA Today earlier this year after the International Olympic Committee barred trans athletes from competing in their chosen gender category. Turner argued the conversation "distracts from the structural inequities that actually limit women's opportunities."The WNBA players' association has also gone on record in support of trans inclusion, stating in a 2022 release that banning trans kids from sports "ostracizes them from a fundamental part of growing up." The Fever organization had not commented on Cunningham's remarks as of publication.This isn't the first time Cunningham has waded into the topic. In 2022, she shared a since-deleted tweet from CBS Sports host Josh Pate, who wrote that "Female collegiate athletes deserve so much better than this" regarding transgender swimmer Lia Thomas' run on Penn's women's team. Cunningham followed with her own statement on X, saying she stands with "all people, especially my fellow athletes," and voicing support for people of all backgrounds competing fairly.Indiana's own scholastic and collegiate transgender athlete ban was upheld by the Supreme Court in late June, adding another layer to the debate playing out around Cunningham's team. Whether the Fever organization issues a formal response, or whether Cunningham's comments follow her into the All-Star break, remains an open question as Indiana closes out the first half of its season.
Article Summary: Sophie Cunningham is one of the WNBA's most recognizable and polarizing players. ESPN published a profile on the Indiana Fever guard July 21 that dug into her views on transgender participation in women's sports. Cunningham told ESPN she has been mischaracterized as anti-trans, saying her goal is protecting girls and young women in athletics.

Article Title: “One more chip in you”: Stephen Curry’s wife Ayesha Curry makes candid confession about how she inspires Warriors legend for another title run
Article Text:
Stephen Curry’ and Ayesha Curry. Image via: Taylor Hill/Getty Images

Ayesha Curry reveals how she keeps Stephen Curry inspired

Ayesha Curry to star in a romantic comedy

Ayesha Curry celebrated Stephen Curry's ESPYs win

Last season, Stephen Curry was riddled with injuries in the final moments of the Warriors' run, but he’s not done. Ayesha Curry says she has one message for her husband heading into another Golden State Warriors season: one more championship is still in him. The actress and entrepreneur shared that sentiment in a joint interview with The Hollywood Reporter, days after Stephen picked up the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award at the 2026 ESPYs for his work with the Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation.The comments land in the middle of a packed summer for the couple. Stephen is rehabbing from last season's knee injury and weighing a contract extension, while Ayesha steps into her first lead film role in Roku's holiday rom-com. Together, the pair are also set to be named Philanthropists of the Year at The Hollywood Reporter's Social Impact Summit.Stephen missed 27 straight games last season because of a knee injury, and he told The Hollywood Reporter the setback changed how he trains and how urgently he treats the time he has left on the court. He said he doesn't dwell on his basketball mortality, but the injury sharpened his intentionality and pushed him to work smarter at 38 than he did five years ago.Ayesha said she reminds him of his ceiling almost every week. "I just know you have one more chip in you," she told the outlet, adding that she repeats the line to him often. Stephen backed that up by saying he still wants to be the hardest worker in any room he walks into, whatever the season brings.Ayesha is headlining Second Chance Holiday alongside Matthew Daddario for the Roku Channel, playing a dating expert convinced she is cursed to get dumped every holiday season until an old flame resurfaces. It marks her first time topping a film call sheet after supporting roles in Irish Wish, Joy to the World and A Black Lady Sketch Show, plus a voice role in this year's animated hit GOAT."It's an honor to be part of Second Chance Holiday," Curry said in a statement announcing the project. Stephen, meanwhile, has built out Unanimous Media into a full production company under a Comcast NBCUniversal deal, with GOAT grossing close to $200 million and the MLK documentary short The Baddest Speechwriter of All picked up by Netflix after winning at Sundance.Curry became the fourth NBA player to win the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award, following Chris Paul, Kevin Durant and Jrue Holiday, with Ayesha by his side at New York's David H. Koch Theater on July 15. She marked the moment on Instagram with a simple message: "So proud of my baby, always."Accepting the award from Lindsey Vonn, Curry credited Oakland's role in the foundation's growth. "It's about what happens when a community shows up for its kids," he said, pointing to the city where the Warriors won their first three titles together. Eat. Learn. Play., which he and Ayesha launched in 2019, has since expanded access to meals, education and recreation programs for local youth, and the recognition keeps coming: People Magazine reports the couple will join singer John Mayer and actor Michael J. Fox as Philanthropists of the Year at next week's Social Impact Summit.None of it settles the bigger basketball question hanging over Curry's summer. He's already made a personal pitch to free agent LeBron James about joining the Warriors, and his own contract situation, one year remaining, remains unresolved heading into training camp.
Article Summary: Ayesha Curry says she reminds her husband that he has one more championship in him. The comments land in the middle of a packed summer for the couple. Stephen is rehabbing from last season's knee injury and weighing a contract extension. Ayesha steps into her first lead role in Roku's holiday rom-com.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Lao Tzu: 'Time is a created thing. To say ‘I don’t have time,’ is like saying, ‘I don’t want to' and a reality check about priorities
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Lao Tzu

Nothing is rushed in nature

Timeless quote on time

Lao Tzu (also written as Laozi) was an ancient Chinese philosopher and writer, traditionally considered the founder of Taoism (or Daoism). He is believed to have lived around the 6th century BCE, roughly around the same time as Confucius. Legend says he worked as a scholar and keeper of the royal archives for the Zhou dynasty before growing weary of society's corruption, riding off into the west on a water buffalo, and writing the Tao Te Ching, the central text of Taoist thought, before disappearing.Whether Lao Tzu was a single historical person or a title representing a group of wise thinkers, his influence on Eastern philosophy is massive. Taoism focuses on living in harmony with the Tao (the "Way"), which is the natural, flowing order of the universe. Rather than forcing things, fighting against reality, or chasing endless desires, Taoism preaches simplicity, humility, and effortlessly moving with life's natural rhythm."Time is a created thing. To say ‘I don’t have time,’ is like saying, ‘I don’t want to" is one of the most memorable quotes of Lao Tzu because it is ageless. It teaches us something that we encounter every day -- someone giving the excuse of little time and, in fact, clarifying their priority.In modern Westernized culture, we tend to view time as a ticking clock, a rigid, scarce resource that is constantly running out. We divide our days into minutes, set strict schedules, and measure our worth by how productive we are.Lao Tzu and the early Taoists saw the world very differently. In nature, nothing is rushed, yet everything gets done, he said. Seasons change on their own, trees grow without straining, and rivers flow around obstacles without forcing their way through.To a Taoist, nature does not operate on a clock; it operates on presence and priority. When humans invented clocks, calendars, and rigid daily routines, we created the artificial concept of "not having enough time." Lao Tzu’s philosophy reminds us that time isn't a physical monster chasing us: it's a mental construct shaped entirely by what we choose to care about.This quote is a blunt, radical reality check about human agency, responsibility, and priorities.Lao Tzu said time as we understand the concept in not an unyielding physical constraint like gravity. Time is made up of choices. Everyone on Earth gets the same 24 hours in a day. The fabric of a day expands or shrinks based on how we choose to fill it. When you say, "I wish I had time to exercise," or "I don't have time to read," you are acting as if time is an external boss ruling over you. In reality, you are the creator of your schedule. You "create" time for the things you value, and you destroy time for the things you ignore.Not having time for something is a lie. If you don't have time for something, it means you don't want to do that. Lao Tzu strips away our excuses. He points out that saying "I don't have time" is almost always a passive-aggressive translation for:"This isn't a high priority for me right now.""I don't care enough about this to make room for it.""I am unwilling to sacrifice something else to do this."It forces us to own our decisions. If you truly wanted to do something, you would drop something less important to make room for it.While the quote sounds harsh at first, its deeper purpose is actually liberating. If time were truly a rigid cage beyond your control, you would be a helpless victim of your busy life. But if time is something you create, then you hold the keys. You are in complete control of your priorities. Realizing this allows you to stop feeling guilty about the things you don't do. Instead of saying, "I don't have time to learn a language," you can honestly say, "I'm choosing to focus my energy on my career and family right now, so learning a language isn't my priority. " That simple shift restores your personal agency.
Article Summary: Lao Tzu (also written as Laozi) was an ancient Chinese philosopher and writer. He is traditionally considered the founder of Taoism (or Daoism) He is believed to have lived around the 6th century BCE, roughly around the same time as Confucius.

Article Title: Who is Yaxel Lendeborg? All about Summer League championship game MVP as Warriors beat Grizzlies
Article Text:
Yaxel Lendeborg. Image via: Mike Kirschbaum/NBAE via Getty Images

Who is Warriors star Yaxel Lendeborg?

Who are Yaxel Lendeborg's parents?

Yaxel Lendeborg's mom has a huge role in his career

axel Lendeborg and his mom Yissel Raposo. Image via: Yaxel Lendeborg/X

Inside Yaxel Lendeborg's relationship with his father

Yaxel Lendeborg earned Summer League championship game MVP honors after leading the Golden State Warriors past the Memphis Grizzlies 94-90 in the 2026 NBA Summer League title game on Sunday in Las Vegas.The Warriors' first-round pick posted 21 points, 10 rebounds, two assists and two steals, capping a breakout six-game run in Las Vegas that announced him as one of the league's most promising rookies.Golden State trailed by 10 entering the fourth quarter before Lendeborg's late surge sealed the franchise's first Summer League title since 2013.Lendeborg was born in Puerto Rico and starred at the University of Michigan before Golden State selected him No. 11 overall in the 2026 NBA Draft. He arrived at the Summer League title game fresh off a Michigan national championship, and he carried that momentum into Las Vegas, averaging 14.8 points, 7.2 rebounds and 4.0 assists across six appearances.In the championship game itself, Lendeborg shot 9 of 18 from the field and hit a go-ahead three-pointer with just over two minutes remaining, giving the Warriors their first lead since the middle of the third quarter.He followed it with a midrange pull-up that forced a Memphis timeout, then grabbed a critical rebound after the Grizzlies missed a potential go-ahead three in the closing seconds. Deivon Smith added 21 points off the bench, while Memphis got 19 points apiece from Cameron Boozer and Cedric Coward in the loss.Basketball runs in Lendeborg's family. His mother, Yissel Raposo, and father, Okary Lendeborg, both played for the Dominican Republic national basketball team, and Yissel also competed for the country's national volleyball team. She was playing both sports collegiately at the American University of Puerto Rico when Yaxel was born, while his father played professionally overseas. Lendeborg has credited his parents directly for his athletic gifts, telling MLive.com he has natural ability that just comes from my parents.Yissel's influence on her son's path has been constant and, at times, decisive. She bought him NBA gear as a child and recalled him always answering that he wanted to reach the league.When academic struggles made him ineligible to play basketball for two years in high school, Yissel intervened during his senior year, arranging his transfer to a program in Camden, New Jersey, and later using her connections from her playing days in the Dominican system to get him into a camp in New York that led to a scholarship offer from Arizona Western College. Lendeborg has said his mother essentially put him on a plane to Arizona despite his own hesitation.In a February 2026 essay for The Players' Tribune, Lendeborg wrote about his mother's cancer diagnosis and his drive to make her proud, saying he wanted to cut down the nets at Michigan's national championship run most of all for her. He was later seen in tears embracing her after Golden State drafted him, telling ESPN's Lisa Salters he was glad to share that moment with her.Lendeborg has been more guarded about his relationship with his father, describing tension that grew more hostile at points during his upbringing. He has written that his mother tried to help the two find common ground without much success (via PEOPLE ).He has also spoken about leaning on his grandfather, Yissel's father, as an early father figure, and about struggling with motivation after his grandfather's death before his mother helped redirect him toward basketball and school.
Article Summary: Yaxel Lendeborg led the Golden State Warriors past the Memphis Grizzlies 94-90 in the 2026 NBA Summer League title game on Sunday. He posted 21 points, 10 rebounds, two assists and two steals. The Warriors' first-round pick was born in Puerto Rico and starred at the University of Michigan.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Nico Williams: "I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother. Because she once escaped death and…" - an emotional tribute to his mother, the extraordinary journey behind h
Article Text:
Nico Williams (Image: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Nico Williams

"I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother. Because she once escaped death and crossed the Sahara Desert from Ghana to Spain to become who I am today. I gave the medal to my mother because she deserves it more than anyone else"

The moment behind the quote by Nico William

What the comparison is actually saying

Why he gave the medal away

Why sacrifice so often stays invisible behind success

Why this quote still resonates beyond football

A winner's medal usually gets read as proof of what an athlete just achieved on the pitch. Nico Williams, the Spanish footballer, read his differently. "I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother," he said. "Because she once escaped death and crossed the Sahara Desert from Ghana to Spain to become who I am today. I gave the medal to my mother because she deserves it more than anyone else." A medal earned in ninety minutes was, in his own telling, connected to a much longer and harder journey that began decades earlier, long before he was born, on a route that plenty of people crossing it did not survive. His gesture transformed a personal sporting achievement into a powerful tribute to sacrifice, resilience and the countless struggles his family overcame before reaching a safer future.Williams said this after Spain's Euro 2024 victory over England in July 2024, a tournament in which he scored the opening goal of the final and was named one of the standout performers of the whole competition. He was born in Pamplona, Spain, to Ghanaian parents, and his older brother, Iñaki Williams, plays international football for Ghana rather than Spain. The family's migration story, crossing the Sahara before eventually settling in Spain, sits directly behind both brothers' careers.Williams is known specifically for his speed on the pitch, which makes the comparison land harder than it otherwise might. He is not measuring his mother's journey against his own athletic pace literally. He is arguing that the courage and endurance it took her to survive that crossing outweighs anything he has done as a professional footballer, however fast he happens to be able to run.Plenty of athletes dedicate a win to their families in general terms. Williams did something more specific, treating the medal as something connected directly to his mother's own sacrifice rather than solely to his own performance. In his framing, the achievement had more than one author, his training and effort on one side, and the earlier decisions and hardship that made his life in Spain possible on the other.A medal is visible. A goal gets replayed endlessly. The decisions and hardship that came years earlier, the ones that actually made the achievement possible in the first place, rarely get the same attention. Williams' quote pulls that hidden part of the story directly into view, refusing to let his own success stand apart from what his parents went through to give him the chance at it. It serves as a reminder that behind many celebrated sporting moments lies a family's quiet sacrifice, years of persistence, financial struggles and unwavering belief that often remain unseen long after the applause fades.Plenty of people carry family histories shaped by migration and hardship they did not personally experience but still benefit from directly. Williams' quote gives that quiet inheritance a specific, public voice, treating his own visible success as something built on a far less visible journey that came before it.
Article Summary: Nico Williams said this after Spain's Euro 2024 victory over England in July 2024. He was born in Pamplona, Spain, to Ghanaian parents, and his older brother, Iñaki Williams, plays international football for Ghana. The family's migration story, crossing the Sahara before eventually settling in Spain, sits directly behind both brothers' careers.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal: "My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to..." - a powerful lesson on perspective, family sacrifice and why football is not the hardest battle his fa
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal

Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal

"My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to bring food home. That's real pressure. All I have to do is play football"

The moment behind Lamine Yamal’s quote

The family story behind the words

What the comparison is actually doing

Why this reflects more than modesty

Other things Lamine Yamal has said

"It moves me to see my brother so happy, to see my mother living the life she's always dreamed of, and my friends, too. It's a child's greatest dream."

"I'm in love with my little brother. I love him like a son."

Why this still resonates beyond football

Pressure gets discussed constantly in football, usually about missed chances and expectant crowds. Lamine Yamal, Spain's teenage forward, measured his own pressure against something else entirely. "My mother had me when she was 16, that's real pressure," he said. "My father had to figure out life, going out to the street to pick things up. All I have to do is play football." Asked to explain the weight of carrying a national team, he pointed the question straight back at his own family, refusing to let his own circumstances, remarkable as they already were at nineteen, be treated as the hardest thing anyone in his family had actually lived through, given everything his parents had gone through years before he ever kicked a ball professionally.Yamal said this in the build-up to Spain's run at the 2026 World Cup, a tournament Spain went on to win, beating Argentina 1-0 in the final at MetLife Stadium on 19 July, only days after Yamal turned nineteen. Pushed on the pressure of leading the team, he redirected the question entirely: "Don't paint me as if what I'm experiencing is the real pressure. There are people who have endured far more." His response reflected a maturity beyond his age, showing that he viewed football success through the lens of perspective, gratitude and respect for those facing greater struggles.Accordinf to reports, his mother, Sheila Ebana, moved to Catalonia from Bata, Equatorial Guinea, and had Yamal at sixteen. His father, Mounir Nasraoui, arrived in Spain from Morocco as a young child and later worked scrap collection and other temporary jobs to get by. The couple separated when Yamal was around three, and he grew up largely in Rocafonda, a working-class neighbourhood in Mataró, in a small flat where the kitchen and bedroom were effectively one room.Yamal is not describing professional football as easy. Reaching the top of the sport by nineteen required years of relentless training and competing against far older, more experienced players. What he is doing is placing that effort next to a different kind of pressure entirely, the pressure of becoming a parent at sixteen, or of scraping together enough money to keep a family fed. Next to that, he treats his own job, difficult as it genuinely is, as something closer to a privilege than a burden.Public success can easily narrow someone's view until their own difficulties feel like the only ones that matter. Yamal's comment does the opposite. Despite being one of the most closely watched young players in the world, he places his own challenge inside the context of what his parents actually went through to give him the chance at it in the first place.Most people compare their own struggles to someone else's at some point, and it rarely makes the struggle disappear. What it can do is add context. Yamal's own difficulty, performing under enormous public attention at nineteen, is genuinely real. He has simply chosen to measure it against something he considers larger, and decided his own job looks smaller by comparison.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal, Spain's teenage forward, measured his own pressure against something else entirely. Asked to explain the weight of carrying a national team, he pointed the question straight back at his own family. His response reflected a maturity beyond his age, showing that he viewed football success through the lens of perspective, gratitude and respect for those facing greater struggles.

Article Title: Quote of the day by David Beckham: "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to…" - a powerful lesson on hard work, discipline and why talent alone is never
Article Text:
David Beckham

Quote of the day by David Beckham

"I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more"

What is the meaning behind David Beckham’s quote

Why talent alone rarely gets someone all the way there

Why setbacks tested this philosophy directly

Why consistency matters more than occasional motivation

Other famous quotes by David Beckham

"The secret to my success is practice!"

"The secret of the team's success is easy, unity and hard work."

"You will go through tough times, it's about getting through them."

"I don't have time for hobbies. At the end of the day, I treat my job as a hobby. It's something I love doing."

Why it still feels relevant today

People tend to admire success from a safe distance, seeing the trophy without ever seeing the years that produced it. David Beckham summed up his own version of that gap plainly. "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more," he said. The repetition is not accidental. He is describing something continuous, not a single burst of effort that happens once and then stops. Coming from a player often remembered for his technique on free kicks and crosses, the line carries the weight of someone who actually built that skill through repetition, rather than someone simply repeating a motivational slogan he never had to test himself. His journey from a young academy player to a global football icon reflects the discipline, patience and relentless commitment required to turn natural ability into lasting excellence on the biggest stages.The phrase "anything special in life" is deliberately broad, applying just as easily to a career in business or medicine as it does to professional sport. The answer he gives is repeated three times for a reason, work, work, work some more, because Beckham is not describing hard work as an occasional event. He is describing it as the actual, unglamorous rhythm behind lasting achievement.Natural ability offers a real head start, but plenty of gifted people never fully develop it, while plenty of less naturally talented people outwork their way past them over time.Former teammates and coaches have repeatedly described Beckham staying behind after regular training to practise free kicks and crosses long after everyone else had gone home, which is really the practical version of the quote in action.Beckham faced serious public criticism at points in his career, most famously after being sent off during England's 1998 World Cup exit to Argentina. He later described that period bluntly. "I have nightmares about France 98," he said. "It was humiliating. It will always be with me." Rather than letting that moment define the rest of his career, he kept working and went on to become one of England's most recognised footballers, a direct example of the same principle behind today's quote.Motivation naturally rises and falls, strong some days and completely absent on others. Discipline is what keeps someone working through the days motivation does not show up, which is really the difference between an ambition that stays an idea and one that eventually becomes a career.Modern culture often makes success look instant, a single viral moment or a sudden breakthrough. Beckham's own career is a reminder that most of what actually produces lasting results happens quietly, well before anyone else is paying attention, repeated on ordinary days long after the initial excitement of starting has worn off.
Article Summary: "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more" is David Beckham's quote of the day. The phrase is repeated three times for a reason. Beckham is not describing hard work as an occasional event. He is describing it as the actual, unglamorous rhythm behind lasting achievement.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Laozi: 'A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving' and how the best parts of life happen on the detours you never planned
Article Text:
'A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving'

A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving."

Ancient roots and modern meanings

Daodejing

Shan xing wu zhe ji

"A good traveler leaves no tracks or wheel ruts".

Daodejing

The freedom of focusing on the process

wu wei

Focusing on the Goal: Treating the present moment like a stepping stone, causing stress whenever unexpected delays happen.

Treating the present moment like a stepping stone, causing stress whenever unexpected delays happen. Focusing on the Process: Reacting smoothly to changing situations, treating unexpected surprises as useful moments rather than annoying failures.

Living in an unpredictable world

Picture a traveller standing at a busy intersection in a foreign city. Her phone battery dies, taking her maps, digital tickets, and step-by-step plans with it. Instead of panicking, she puts the phone in her pocket, turns down a side street lined with local bakeries, and spends the next three hours exploring an old neighbourhood absent from every guidebook. She has no schedule, no deadline, and no rush to reach a specific landmark.That shift in mindset captures the heart of the famous ancient saying: "The sentence points out a basic problem in how people live their lives. Most people plan their lives around specific destinations, whether a physical city, a job title, or a savings goal. By doing this, they turn the journey into a series of hurdles to clear as quickly as possible. The phrase suggests a totally different approach: treating life as an experience to enjoy directly rather than a set list of tasks to complete.The phrase "A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving" comes from Chapter 27 of the(Tao Te Ching), an ancient Chinese book written around the 4th century BCE.While tradition says the book was written by the wise thinker Laozi, the exact English words used today come from Stephen Mitchell's popular 1988 translation. Scholars of ancient Chinese point out that Mitchell’s version is a creative interpretation rather than a strict line-by-line translation. The original Chinese phrase,, translates more literally to:In ancient China, a traveller who left no tracks was a master of living in balance. They moved so naturally through a landscape that they caused no damage, left no heavy mark, and easily adapted to whatever terrain appeared. Mitchell translated that deep spiritual idea of smooth movement into everyday words, swapping the image of "leaving no tracks" for the idea of dropping strict plans.The historical background of theexplains why this idea mattered so much. Written during a time of constant war and political trouble in China, the book offered a direct challenge to the strict social rules and rigid government systems of the time. Laozi argued that trying to force the world into strict plans only causes stress and pain. True success, whether in ruling a country or living daily life, meant going with the natural flow of the world.At its heart, this idea relies on the concept of, often translated as "effortless action" or "going with the flow." It describes a mental state where a person reacts to real life as it actually happens, rather than trying to force events to fit a set plan.In modern psychology, this matches research on "flow states"—moments of deep focus where a person forgets about time and becomes fully wrapped up in what they are doing. When someone focuses only on a distant goal, their mind gets split. Part of their brain stays trapped in worry about the future, constantly checking how far they are from the finish line.Focusing only on the end result creates a common mental trap:By letting go of the need for a specific final result, a person stops treating today as a task to rush through to get to tomorrow.This ancient idea offers real value across modern life, especially in a world full of strict schedules and tight deadlines.In business and leadership, rigid long-term plans often fail when situations change fast. The most successful organizations work less like train drivers stuck on a single set of tracks and more like sailors adjusting their sails to changing winds. Modern business practices use this exact idea: keeping a clear general direction while remaining ready to drop old plans when real-world conditions change.In personal development and education, an obsession with fixed goals often leads to exhaustion and burnout. Students and workers who treat their careers as a single, inflexible path struggle when unexpected problems appear. Those who approach their work like flexible travellers treat surprising detours as chances to build new skills and find unexpected interests.Using this idea in daily life does not mean throwing away all goals or living in complete chaos. It simply means holding plans loosely. It means picking a direction while staying open to the path, knowing that the most meaningful experiences often happen on the unplanned detours.
Article Summary: "A good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving" comes from Chapter 27 of the(Tao Te Ching), an ancient Chinese book written around the 4th century BCE. The phrase suggests treating life as an experience to enjoy directly rather than a set list of tasks to complete.

Article Title: Prince George turns 13: Prince William and Kate Middleton release new birthday photo and beach video ahead of his Eton College debut
Article Text:
Prince George turns 13: Prince William and Kate Middleton release new birthday photo and beach video ahead of his Eton College debut

New beach video offers a glimpse into family life

Preparing for life at Eton College

Prince George has officially entered his teenage years. Prince William and Kate Middleton marked their eldest son's 13th birthday by sharing a new portrait and a heartwarming video on social media, celebrating the milestone just weeks before he begins a new chapter at Eton College.Taking to their official Instagram account on Wednesday, the Prince and Princess of Wales posted a smiling portrait of George, captured by photographer Matt Porteous. In the picture, the young royal is seen dressed in a dark suit paired with an open-neck white shirt, with his hands casually tucked into his pockets."Happy 13th Birthday, George! 🎉," the royal couple captioned the post.Alongside the birthday portrait, Kensington Palace also released a candid video featuring George enjoying time outdoors with his family.The footage shows the teenager walking along a beach, petting the family's dog, playing cricket on the sand and climbing rocks barefoot alongside his younger brother, Prince Louis. In one clip, George is also seen smiling and waving at the camera while wearing sunglasses.Sharing the video, the Prince and Princess of Wales wrote, "Thank you for all the birthday wishes for George today!"The behind-the-scenes montage was filmed during the family's Easter holiday in Cornwall and offers a rare glimpse into the private lives of the Wales family.George's birthday celebrations come just days after he attended the Wimbledon Championships with Prince William and Princess Charlotte. The trio were seen enjoying the tennis action together, continuing a tradition of royal appearances at the iconic sporting event.The birthday also marks the beginning of an important transition for Prince George. In September, he will begin secondary school at the prestigious Eton College in Windsor, following in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.Although it has not been confirmed whether George will board at the school, Eton is located only a few miles from the family's home in Windsor, allowing flexibility for the future king's education.George has until now studied at Lambrook School in Berkshire, where his younger siblings, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis, continue to attend.Born as George Alexander Louis at St Mary's Hospital in London in July 2013, Prince George was third in line to the British throne at birth. Following the accession of King Charles III, he is now second in line after his father, Prince William.
Article Summary: Prince George turns 13 on Wednesday. Prince William and Kate Middleton shared a new portrait and a video on social media. George will begin secondary school at Eton College in September. He will follow in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.

Article Title: Scientists found millions of fish nests stretching across the seafloor like a vast city, how underwater robot 'Lassie' helped in the discovery beneath the ice of Antarctica’s Weddell Sea
Article Text:
Representative Image

"Lassie,"

"Lassie"

How the iceberg exposed the hidden ecosystem

"Lassie"

Yellowfin notie fish build and guard the nests

"selfish herd"

Expedition also searched for Shackleton's Endurance

Findings could support conservation efforts

Scientists have uncovered a hidden fish breeding ground in Antarctica. The discovery was made beneath the ice of Antarctica's Weddell Sea after the A68 iceberg broke away from the Larsen C Ice Shelf in 2017. This incident exposed the seabed that had been covered by around 200 metres of ice. Using underwater robots, including the remotely operated vehicle (ROV)researchers documented thousands of organised fish nests, adding to evidence of a much larger breeding habitat stretching across the seafloor.The discovery was made during the 2019 Weddell Sea Expedition, which employed autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) and the remotely operated vehicleto explore the newly accessible area. The researchers found more than 1,000 circular nests in organised formations, each cleared of plankton debris covering the surrounding seabed. The findings support previous studies that indicate the area is one of the largest known fish breeding colonies in the world.The opportunity to study the region arose after the A68 iceberg, which covered about 5,800 square kilometres, calved from the Larsen C Ice Shelf in 2017. The event opened access to a section of the seafloor that had previously remained hidden beneath the ice shelf.Scientists aboard the South African polar research vessel SA Agulhas II used AUVs and the ROVto survey the seabed. Images captured by the underwater vehicles showed circular nests arranged in geometric clusters rather than randomly scattered across the seafloor.Each nest had been cleared of phytoplankton detritus, suggesting active maintenance by the fish.Researchers identified the nest builders as yellowfin notie (Lindbergichthys nudifrons), a species of Antarctic rockcod. Each nest is guarded by an adult fish that protects its eggs from predators.According to the expedition, the nesting pattern may reflect thetheory, in which fish nesting near the centre of the colony gain additional protection from predators. At the same time, larger individuals occupy nests along the edges, where defence is more challenging.The 2019 Weddell Sea Expedition had two objectives: conducting scientific research near the Larsen C Ice Shelf and searching for Sir Ernest Shackleton's ship, Endurance, which sank in 1915.Although heavy sea ice prevented the team from locating the wreck during the expedition, the experience gained from operating underwater vehicles in difficult conditions contributed to the Endurance22 mission, which located the wreck in March 2022 at a depth of 3,008 metres.Scientists say the nesting grounds provide further evidence that the region contains a Vulnerable Marine Ecosystem that supports Antarctic biodiversity. The findings also expand on work published by Purser and colleagues in 2022 that found one of the largest known fish breeding colonies on Earth in the Weddell Sea.Researchers say documenting these underwater habitats could help proposals to make the Weddell Sea a Marine Protected Area, conserving breeding grounds that are part of Antarctica’s marine ecosystem.
Article Summary: Discovery was made beneath the ice of Antarctica's Weddell Sea after the A68 iceberg broke away from the Larsen C Ice Shelf in 2017. This incident exposed the seabed that had been covered by around 200 metres of ice. Using underwater robots, scientists documented thousands of organised fish nests, adding to evidence of a much larger breeding habitat stretching across the seafloor.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

RCB register their second win

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: 'I had bruises all over my body': Some women cops in harm’s way during CJP protest
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Several women police officers, posted at and around Jantar Mantar on July 20, said they were injured during the Parliament march called by Cockroach Janta Party. Some alleged that stones were thrown at them.Shristi Pandey, an IPS officer, was stationed near Kerala House around 12.30 pm when the protesters tried to breach a three-layer barricade. She said the students kept pushing the barricade to make way for the march, while police tried to hold the structure in place. She said the massive force caused injuries to her abdomen and shoulders.“At one point, the protesters started pushing police against the barricades. Several personnel were injured. I was also hurt,” Pandey said.When she was asked about police high-handedness, an allegation many protesters have made, Pandey said: “If we had intended to use a heavy-handed approach, we would have done so in the morning itself. We continuously appealed to them to maintain peace.”Additional CP Nupur Prasad said someone threw stones from the roof of a public washroom while police were stopping protesters from moving towards Sansad Marg between noon and 12.30 pm.Prasad’s colleague, Monika Bhardwaj, was injured when protesters allegedly threw stones at police near Sansad Marg at 11.30 am and at Janpath around 4 pm. Bhardwaj suffered injuries on her hands and feet. Her operator was struck in the face by a flying brick. “I had bruises all over my body,” Bhardwaj said. “My left hand was red from the impact.”Another IPS officer, Niharika Bhatt, said the protesters at Sansad Marg threw stones, flower pots, shoes and slippers at police. They uprooted paving stones at Janpath and hurled those. The protesters used pepper spray on the cops, she said.Bhatt also had injuries on her hands and feet. “We had been engaging with protesters since 6 am,” she said.Additional DCP Aishwarya Singh said around 4.20 pm, at the Tolstoy Road-Janpath area, stone throwing started. Several in her team were hurt. She suffered an injury on her left foot.They remained defensive till reinforcements came, after which police used force to control the situation.
Article Summary: Several women police officers, posted at and around Jantar Mantar, said they were injured during the Parliament march called by Cockroach Janta Party. Some alleged that stones were thrown at them. Shristi Pandey, an IPS officer, was stationed near Kerala House when the protesters tried to breach a three-layer barricade.

Article Title: 4 held for theft of 17 kg silver mask, cladding of Shivling at historic temple in Maharashtra's Khopoli
Article Text:
Khopoli police arrested four burglars for stealing silver items from a historic temple

NAVI MUMBAI: The Khopoli police in Raigad detected the burglary at the 18th century historic Shree Vishveshwar temple, where 17 kg silver mask and silver cladding on Shivling was stolen on July 19 past midnight around 2 am, by arresting four accused burglars on Tuesday.The arrested accused are Amol Kasbe (36) and Vikas Agiwale (25), who were arrested from Shirur, Pune district and Dilip Jadhav (40) and Sakharam Gaikwad (40), who were arrested from Parner in Ahilyanagar district.The 17 kg silver mask and cladding on the Shivling was adorned in 2023 for which the around Rs 28 lakhs expenses were borne by the temple's trust from the donations received from devotees.Sachin Hire, senior inspector of Khopoli said, "On July 19, the assistant priest at the temple identified as Kishore Shigwan (56) lodge a complaint that the silver mask and cladding on Shivling was stolen. The thieves had entered the temple by breaking the chain and lock of the rear gate, which always remained close as the devotees were allowed entry only from the front gate.""Upon checking CCTV camera footage at the temple, it was seen that four masked men had entered the sanctum sanctorum around 2 am and decamped with the silver mask and cladding on the Shivling. Subsequently, an FIR was registered under BNS sections for house tres-pass, theft in a dwelling house and joint criminal liability. "Inspector Hire said, "Multiple teams were formed to trace the gang of burglars.Using technical expertise and informer network, we zeroed-in on four history-sheeters. They were traced to Pune and Ahilyanagar district and gradually apprehended on Tuesday.""They have revealed the place where they have hidden the stolen silver mask and cladding. Accordingly, a police team will recover the stolen valuables. Previously, there are criminal cases of theft and robbery registered against the arrested accused in Ahilyanagar and Pune districts."
Article Summary: The 17 kg silver mask and cladding on the Shivling was adorned in 2023 for which the around Rs 28 lakhs expenses were borne by the temple's trust. The arrested accused are Amol Kasbe (36), Vikas Agiwale (25), Dilip Jadhav (40) and Sakharam Gaikwad (40), who were arrested from Parner in Ahilyanagar district.
